text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听Real Easy English。

text: In this podcast, we have real conversations in easy English to help you learn.
zh: 在这个播客里，我们用简单的英语进行真实的对话，帮助你学习。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是Neil。

text: And I'm Beth.
zh: 我是Beth。

text: You can find a video version of this podcast and a free worksheet to help you learn on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到这期播客的视频版本和免费的学习讲义。

text: Hi Beth, how are you?
zh: 嗨，Beth，你好吗？

text: I'm good, Neil, how are you?
zh: 我很好，Neil，你呢？

text: Very well, thank you.
zh: 我也很好，谢谢。

text: What are we talking about today?
zh: 我们今天聊什么话题？

text: we are talking about housemates.
zh: 我们要聊聊室友。

text: Ah, housemates.
zh: 啊，室友。

text: The people that you share a house with who aren't your family.
zh: 就是和你同住一个房子、但不是你家人的人。

text: Maybe they're your friends or even strangers who just need somewhere to live.
zh: 可能是你的朋友，甚至是只是需要个住处的陌生人。

text: Exactly.
zh: 没错。

text: And it's quite common in the UK to live with housemates when you're younger.
zh: 在英国，年轻的时候和室友一起住是很常见的。

text: Have you ever lived with housemates?
zh: 你有过室友吗？

text: Yes, of course.
zh: 当然有。

text: I had house or flatmates when I was a student and then after I was a student in my first jobs and before I got married.
zh: 我上学的时候有过室友或合租的舍友，后来工作、结婚前也有过。

text: That's exactly the same as me.
zh: 我也是一样。

text: So when I went to university, I moved in with five people.
zh: 我上大学的时候，搬进去和五个人一起住。

text: And we all had a separate room and a separate bathroom.
zh: 我们每个人都有自己的房间和独立的卫生间。

text: But yeah, they were my flatmates for my first year of university.
zh: 不过在大一那年，他们算是我的合租舍友。

text: But in my second year, then I moved into a house and we all shared a bathroom and they were my housemates because we were in a house.
zh: 到了大二，我搬进了一栋房子，我们共用一个卫生间，那时他们就是我的室友，因为我们住在同一栋房子里。

text: Did you enjoy having housemates?
zh: 你喜欢有室友的生活吗？

text: I think it really depends on the people that you live with.
zh: 我觉得这真的取决于和你一起住的人。

text: I have had some bad housemates.
zh: 我遇到过一些糟糕的室友。

text: Bad because they weren't tidy, didn't clean,
zh: 糟糕是因为他们不整洁，不打扫卫生，

text: there were dishes in the kitchen that were dirty and not cleaned for a long time um but i've also had some really nice housemates
zh: 厨房里的脏碗盘放了很久都不洗，不过我也遇到过一些非常好的室友。

text: as well do you enjoy living with housemates or did you enjoy living with housemates i think as you said it
zh: 那你呢，你喜欢或曾经喜欢和室友一起住吗？我觉得就像你说的，

text: really depends on the person so you mentioned people being messy and that kind of thing also noisy easy people are difficult.
zh: 这真的取决于人，你提到有人邋遢之类的，还有吵闹的人也很难相处。

text: But some of my best friends now have been my housemates in the past.
zh: 不过我现在最好的一些朋友，以前都是我的室友。

text: So I think you can really get to know people very well.
zh: 所以我觉得你真的可以借此非常了解一个人。

text: And you can also meet some people who you never want to see again in your life.
zh: 你也可能遇到一些这辈子都不想再见的人。

text: That's true.
zh: 确实如此。

text: So, Neil, what do you think is the best kind of personality, the best kind of person to live with?
zh: 那Neil，你觉得什么样的性格、什么样的人最适合一起住？

text: They have to be easygoing.
zh: 他们必须随和一点。

text: What does that mean?
zh: 这是什么意思？

text: Somebody who is easygoing is relaxed and they don't get too upset or angry over little things.
zh: 随和的人性格放松，不会为一点小事就生气或不高兴。

text: things.
zh: （同上，衔接词）

text: And they're quite easy to live with.
zh: 而且他们很容易相处。

text: And there's the clue, easygoing.
zh: 这就是提示所在——随和。

text: I think something else important about living with other people or renting somewhere, so paying monthly, is having a good landlord.
zh: 我觉得和别人同住，或者租房子按月付钱，还有一件很重要的事，就是要有一个好房东。

text: Very important.
zh: 非常重要。

text: So who is a landlord?
zh: 那房东是谁呢？

text: What is a landlord?
zh: 房东是什么意思？

text: So the landlord is, or landlady, is the person that owns the house or flat that you're in and you pay them rent.
zh: 房东，或者说女房东，是拥有你所住的房子或公寓的人，你要付租金给他。

text: So you pay them a monthly amount, but you might live there for a year or two years or three years, but you don't own the place.
zh: 你每个月付一定的钱，可能你会住一年、两年或三年，但你并不拥有这个地方。

text: It's the landlord that owns the place.
zh: 是房东拥有这个地方。

text: Yeah, and a good landlord can make a big difference.
zh: 是的，一个好房东真的能带来很大的不同。

text: Neil, are you easy to live with?
zh: Neil，你是那种容易相处的人吗？

text: Should we be flatmates?
zh: 我们可以做合租舍友吗？

text: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

text: But I think I have made some good friends when I've been a housemate.
zh: 不过我觉得当我是室友的时候，交到了一些好朋友。

text: So they think that I was a good housemate.
zh: 所以他们应该觉得我是个不错的室友。

text: Okay.
zh: 好吧。

text: How about you?
zh: 那你呢？

text: Yeah, I think I'm easy to live with.
zh: 我觉得我挺好相处的。

text: But I like to sing a lot.
zh: 不过我很喜欢唱歌。

text: So maybe people would tell me to be quiet.
zh: 所以可能有人会让我安静点。

text: Although no one's ever told me to be quiet, but maybe they wanted me to, just didn't say.
zh: 虽然从没人真的这么说过，但也许他们心里想让我安静，只是没说出口。

text: Let's recap the vocabulary we've heard in this podcast, starting with housemate.
zh: 我们来回顾一下这期播客中出现的词汇，先从housemate（室友）开始。

text: A housemate is someone you live with who probably isn't part of your family.
zh: housemate指的是和你同住、但可能不是你家人的人。

text: We also hear flatmate.
zh: 我们还听到了flatmate（合租舍友）。

text: And we had easygoing, which means relaxed and not easily upset.
zh: 还有easygoing，意思是性格随和、不容易生气。

text: Flatmates or housemates often rent a property and that means they don't own it, they pay money to the person who does own it.
zh: 合租舍友或室友通常是租房住，也就是说他们并不拥有房产，而是付钱给拥有房产的人。

text: And the person who owns the house or flat is the landlord.
zh: 拥有这栋房子或公寓的人就是房东。

text: That's who you pay every month and they own the property.
zh: 你每个月付钱给的就是他，而他拥有这套房产。

text: That's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 这就是本期Real Easy English的全部内容。

text: to test what you've learnt, there's a worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 想检验一下自己学到了什么，可以到我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找讲义。

text: And we'll be back next week with another Easy English Conversation.
zh: 我们下周再见，带来另一期简单英语对话节目。

text: Bye for now!
zh: 再见！

text: Bye!
zh: 再见！
